title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds recoverable multipart-upload state machine, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of recoverable multipart-upload state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
Persist upload ID, part number, offset, size, checksum, ETag, attempt, and owner so completion uses the exact accepted part list.
Bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent goroutine or buffer leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Multipart Threshold Part Size
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 2309 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Multipart Threshold Part Size.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Multipart Threshold Part Size, keep Multipart Threshold Part Size connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
